channel.title: April 2026 Curations on Junho Lee
channel.description: Recent content in April 2026 Curations on Junho Lee

item.title: Prompt Master: Engineering Waste-Free Claude Workflows
item.description: Reduce AI retries and token waste using a structured framework that optimizes prompts across nine technical dimensions and specific model architectures.

item.title: Optimizing RAG Accuracy Through Structural Data Engineering
item.description: Improve RAG retrieval quality by preserving document hierarchy and layout context through smart chunking and Markdown conversion.

item.title: Scrapling: Adaptive Web Scraping That Survives UI Changes
item.description: Scrapling tracks elements by similarity rather than fixed selectors, bypasses anti-bot detection natively, and connects to AI agents via MCP – built for web environments that change without warning.

item.title: Hermes WebUI: Browser-Based Control for Autonomous Agents
item.description: Hermes WebUI moves autonomous agent management from the terminal to the browser – multi-profile context isolation, file browsing, and long-term memory in one interface.

item.title: Managing a Stock Portfolio from the Terminal with firma
item.description: firma replaces spreadsheet-based portfolio tracking with a local SQLite store, Finnhub price feeds, and MCP integration for Claude Desktop – letting you query your investments in plain language.

item.title: ChatGPT Personalized Reports as Shareable Image Cards
item.description: One photo or one conversation log is enough for ChatGPT to produce a personalized report as a shareable image card. A collection of prompts you can use right now.

item.title: What Actually Improves Claude Code Performance: Configuration and Architecture
item.description: Keeping Claude Code minimal, forcing full reasoning with three settings.json lines, and investing in system architecture — a practical take on what actually moves the needle.

item.title: 32 Slash Command Shortcuts That LLMs Instantly Understand
item.description: 32 slash commands that work out of the box with Claude, ChatGPT, and Gemini – no custom definitions needed. Categorized by use case with practical combination strategies.

item.title: Analyzing the Official GitHub Repository of a Major Korean Brokerage Open API
item.description: A structural analysis of the official sample code from a major Korean brokerage API, optimized for LLM agents and Python environments.

item.title: macshot: A Native macOS Tool Emerging as an Alternative to Paid Apps
item.description: A look at macshot, a native open-source macOS screenshot and screen recording tool that delivers powerful features without the subscription burden.

item.title: OpenDocuments: A Local RAG Platform That Unifies Fragmented Team Knowledge
item.description: An open-source platform that connects documents scattered across Notion, GitHub, and S3, then queries them with a local LLM. Runs entirely on-premise without external APIs.

item.title: Thinking Like an Agent Conductor in the AI Era
item.description: Even after adopting agents, if the work structure itself does not change, productivity does not scale. Reid Hoffman’s conductor strategy starts by breaking that illusion.

item.title: Using Korean Legal Data for AI Agent Development via Beopmang API
item.description: Parsing raw legal XML means broken table structures and half a day lost on preprocessing. Beopmang is an API that delivers pre-cleaned JSON, solving that problem upfront.

item.title: Optimizing Large-Scale Excel Downloads in Spring Boot
item.description: A Spring Boot library for streaming 1M-row Excel downloads without OOM. A single annotation eliminates the boilerplate.

item.title: OpenDocuments: A Self-Hosted RAG Platform Connecting Scattered Team Knowledge
item.description: A self-hosted RAG platform that runs on Ollama to unify team documents scattered across Notion, GitHub, and S3. Natural language search works even in air-gapped environments without external APIs.
